archetype: ARCHETYPE 2: THE PERFORMANCE PRO (Adapted for Technical Audio Studio)

typography.instructions: Use Manrope for all headings with tracking-tight (e.g., tracking-tight or tracking-tighter). Use JetBrains Mono for timecodes, frequency scales, transcriptions, and numerical data. Do NOT use Inter, Space Grotesk, or generic Sans fonts.

colors.instructions: Tint grays with a slight cool blue. Use Electric Blaze (#FF3B30) exclusively for recording triggers. Volt Blue (#007AFF) for processing actions. Use sharp contrast.

visual_enhancers.instructions: Expose the skeleton of the UI. Use a border-collapse style layout where sections are divided by 1px solid borders (border-white/10). No soft shadows on cards. Use pure flat colors for surfaces. Add neon glows (drop-shadow) ONLY to active states like the recording waveform.

grids_layouts.instructions: Use a 12-column grid structure (grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-12) with tight gaps (gap-4). Never center-everything. Align controls to the left.
structure: Controls Panel: col-span-12 md:col-span-3 (Record, Play, Process toggles)
Visualizer Canvas: col-span-12 md:col-span-9 (Large waveform and spectrogram views)
Transcription & Export Panel: col-span-12 (Bottom section displaying the generated text and download buttons)

components.custom_instructions: Do not use generic HTML <audio> players. Build a custom transport bar (Play, Pause, Stop) using Shadcn buttons that control the waveform visualizer state.

motion.instructions: Use pulse animations on the record button when active. Provide micro-interactions on all hover states (ensure text/icon color contrasts adequately). DO NOT use 'transition: all'.

accessibility.instructions: Ensure 4.5:1 contrast for all text. Active focus states must have a high-contrast ring. DO NOT use transparent backgrounds for surfaces containing text without backdrop-blur.
accessibility.testing: All interactive and informational elements MUST include a data-testid attribute (e.g., data-testid='record-button').

images.description: Studio Microphone in front of monitor
images.category: Hero Banner / Empty State Background Image

instructions_to_main_agent: Build a single-page React app (using .js/.jsx, NOT .tsx) focused on professional audio cleanup and transcription.
Theme must be strictly dark mode, matching a technical audio workstation.
Integrate wavesurfer.js for both waveform rendering and real-time spectrogram visualization.
Implement the UI with a technical grid aesthetic: visible 1px borders dividing panels, sharp corners (rounded-none or max rounded-sm), and flat dense surfaces.
Do not center-align the main application container. Maintain a left-aligned, high-density layout.
Include a before/after split or toggle for the audio to showcase the 5-8KHz conversion and noise removal.
Add prominent download buttons for the processed WAV/MP3 and the text transcription.
Use 'sonner' for all async operation toasts (recording started, processing complete, error states).
Assign 'data-testid' to every control (buttons, sliders) for automated testing.
UNIVERSAL_GUIDELINES_FOR_MAIN_AGENT: You tend to converge toward generic, 'on distribution' outputs. Avoid this AI slop aesthetic. Create distinctive frontends.
Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients.
Don't make generic centered layouts, simplistic gradients, and uniform styling.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy.
Use glass-morphism effects with backdrop filters (12-24px blur) only where necessary.
You MUST NOT apply universal transition (e.g., transition: all). Apply specific transitions.
You MUST NOT center align the app container (.App { text-align: center; }).
Always use modern button styles (sharp or slightly rounded) with clear interaction animations.
NEVER use AI assistant Emoji characters (like 🤖, 🧠, etc.) for icons. Use standard icon libraries.
Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Cramped designs look cheap.
Subtle grain textures or noise overlays can separate good from extraordinary.
Prioritize using pre-existing components from src/components/ui when applicable.
Components MUST use named exports. Pages MUST use default exports.
